qa_pairs.question: Who was the intended audience for the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The intended audience for the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar included prospective users of the upgraded systems and users planning to utilize a substantial amount of H100 resources (e.g., more than one GPU at a time, or over 24 hours runtime).

qa_pairs.question: What was the duration and format of the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' event?
qa_pairs.answer: The 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' was a 1-hour presentation.

qa_pairs.question: What resources were made available from the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: A presentation is available at [https://youtu.be/pxY3G3BhwyA](https://youtu.be/pxY3G3BhwyA) and slides are available at [https://helpwiki.sharcnet.ca/wiki/images/1/1d/MIG_MPS.pdf](https://helpwiki.sharcnet.ca/wiki/images/1/1d/MIG_MPS.pdf).

qa_pairs.question: What practical information was discussed during the 'Survival guide for the upcoming GPU upgrades' webinar?
qa_pairs.answer: The discussion covered how to determine which approach would work best for specific code, and a live demonstration was provided.

qa_pairs.question: Will my data be automatically copied to its new system during the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: No, data migration to the new systems is the responsibility of each National Host Site, which will inform you of what you need to do.
